title: Verses In An Album.
content:
Far above the hollow
    Tempest, and its moan,
    Singeth bright Apollo
    In his golden zone, - 
    Cloud doth never shade him,
    Nor a storm invade him,
    On his joyous throne.

    So when I behold me
    In an orb as bright,
    How thy soul doth fold me
    In its throne of light!
    Sorrow never paineth,
    Nor a care attaineth
    To that blessed height.
meaning:
Thomas Hood's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Verses In An Album."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
